llm_extracted.ai_arm_selection_rationale: Only one AI arm (GitHub Copilot access) vs. control (no Copilot access). This is a between-subjects randomized controlled trial.
llm_extracted.notes: The primary statistic is from a pooled W-IV (weak-instrument IV) regression across three experiments. The 26.08% effect is reported both as a percent increase and as the W-IV coefficient. The outcome is pull requests per week (count/throughput), so larger is better. The regression coefficient appears to be in percentage points (26.08 with SE 10.3), matching the 26.08% increase reported in the effect_in_outcome_units field. Without knowing if the outcome was log-transformed or is in levels, and without a baseline mean, we cannot directly convert the coefficient to a log ratio. Setting regression_outcome_is_log=false because the paper does not indicate log transformation of the outcome variable. Python will need to handle the conversion using the percent change (0.2608) or attempt log(1 + coef/baseline) if a baseline can be inferred elsewhere.
source_quotes: when data is combined across three experiments and 4,867 developers, our analysis reveals a 26.08% increase (SE: 10.3%) in completed tasks among developers using the AI tool.
Developers randomly selected to receive access to GitHub Copilot.
Developers who did not have access to GitHub Copilot during the experimental period.
